name: Contrast Bath Therapy
instructions: Prepare two tubs or buckets of water: one with hot water (38°C to 40°C) and one with cold water (10°C to 15°C).
Immerse the affected body part in the hot water for 3 to 4 minutes.
Immediately transfer the body part to the cold water and immerse for 1 minute.
Repeat this alternating cycle 3 to 5 times.
Always end the session with immersion in the cold water to help minimize residual inflammation.

name: Cold Compress / Ice Pack
instructions: Wrap an ice pack or a sealed bag of crushed ice in a thin towel.
Apply the wrapped ice pack directly to the injured or painful area.
Leave it in place for 15 to 20 minutes.
Remove the ice pack and allow the skin temperature to return to normal before reapplying, if necessary.

name: TENS (Transcutaneous Electrical Nerve Stimulation)
instructions: Ensure the skin is clean and dry where the electrodes will be placed.
Place the TENS unit pads on the skin around the area of pain, ensuring they are at least 1 inch apart.
Turn on the TENS unit and slowly increase the intensity until you feel a strong but comfortable tingling sensation.
Run the therapy session for the prescribed duration (usually 15-30 minutes).
Turn off the unit before removing the electrodes from the skin.

name: Therapeutic Ultrasound
instructions: Apply a generous amount of conductive gel to the treatment area.
Set the ultrasound machine to the prescribed frequency and intensity.
Place the transducer head flat against the skin.
Move the transducer head continuously in slow, overlapping circles over the treatment area.
Continue the treatment for the prescribed duration (typically 5 to 10 minutes).

name: Joint Mobilization (Grades I-II)
instructions: Position the patient comfortably with the joint in an open-packed or resting position.
Support the surrounding limb to isolate the target joint.
Apply rhythmic, oscillating movements (small amplitude for Grade I, larger amplitude for Grade II) within the early range of motion.
Ensure the movements are smooth and pain-free.
Continue for 1-2 minutes per plane of motion.

name: Deep Tissue Friction Massage
instructions: Identify the specific area of adhesion or scar tissue.
Apply deep, firm pressure across the fibers of the target tissue using fingers or thumbs.
Maintain consistent pressure while moving back and forth (preventing skin slipping).
Communicate with the patient about pressure comfort (it should be perceived as a 'good pain').
Continue for 5-10 minutes based on tissue response.

name: Trigger Point Dry Needling
instructions: Identify the active myofascial trigger point through palpation.
Clean the skin over the target area.
Insert a sterile monofilament needle directly into the trigger point.
Observe for a local twitch response.
Leave the needle in place for a short duration or manipulate it to elicit further responses before removal.

name: Kinesiology Taping (Knee Support)
instructions: Ensure the skin is clean, dry, and free of oils or lotions.
Position the knee in slight flexion.
Apply an anchor strip below the patella with 0% tension.
Stretch the tape to 50% tension and apply it around the lateral border of the patella, anchoring above.
Repeat the process around the medial border of the patella.
Rub the tape firmly to activate the adhesive.

name: Foam Rolling (IT Band)
instructions: Lie on your side with the foam roller positioned under the outside of your thigh (IT band).
Support your upper body weight on your forearm.
Slowly roll from just below the hip down to just above the knee.
When you find a tender spot, pause and hold the pressure for 20-30 seconds until the discomfort lessens.
Continue rolling for 1-2 minutes per side.

name: Moist Heat Pack (Hot Compress)
instructions: Prepare a moist heat pack or soak a towel in hot water.
Ensure the temperature is hot but comfortable, wrapping the pack in extra layers of towel to prevent burns.
Apply the heat pack to the stiff or aching area.
Leave in place for 15 to 20 minutes.
Check the skin periodically to ensure it is not becoming too red or irritated.
